Ad Text:
The opportunity

This is an excellent opportunity for an energetic, dynamic Commercial Analyst to join a successful, Aussie company. ASX listed.

Reporting into the Commercial Manager you'll act as a business partner to operational stakeholders, providing commercial, financial & analytical support, to help drive decisions & business performance.

Your remit will include budgeting, forecasting, P&L review, financial modelling and commercial analysis, providing commercial support to department heads, monitoring key performance metrics and other ad hoc analysis.

About you

This role is an excellent role for a Financial Analyst with <3 years experience, or a Financial/Management Accountant with some budgeting/forecasting experience to take on this highly analytical role which will be hands on with implementing and driving change across this large corporate. Strong excel and BI skills is highly regarded.

For more information, please contact Schenuk Wijesekera on 02 9775 5916. Or to apply, hit the "apply" button.

Optus is an Australian telecommunications company, delivering more than 11 million services to our customers every day across mobile, broadband, and digital solutions.

As a Optimisation Manager, we're seeking a passionate and motivated individual to elevate our support to customer experiences, and service delivery.

What you'll be doing

Supporting customers by advocating for their needs.
Lead the development and delivery of initiatives aligned to the strategy and maintaining standards.
Own enterprise governance of policies, standards, and guidelines to ensure consistent and effective implementation.
Drive internal and external engagement and advocacy and continuously improving support outcomes.

What makes you perfect for the role?

A Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience and at least 10 years in broad contact centre and/or Retail operations are required. At least 5 of those years must be in Telecommunications or essential service industries.
Proven expertise in leading teams within fast-paced multinational organisations, with a focus on critical thinking and collaboration.
Strong understanding of business strategy, regulatory laws, and experience in managing and delivering transformation initiatives with demonstrable success.
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, along with highly developed analytical skills and the ability to influence and negotiate at senior levels.

Optus is a place where we let you do you. We offer a range of benefits including:

Competitive remuneration and employee discounts. Make life easier (and more affordable) with $80 monthly credit and 25% off Optus products and unique shopping discounts with our retail partners.
Flexible working arrangements with opportunities to work three days in the office, two days remote or home.
Competitive leave policies, including additional 'Connected' days to focus on culture, family, health, community, or whatever's important to you.
We support growing families with inclusive, carer-neutral paid Parental Leave of up to 16 weeks.
Build meaningful connections through employee-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).
Put your wellbeing first with free access to counselling and support services, 24/7 — in-person, by phone, SMS, or video.

At Optus, we are strengthened by others and that means valuing diversity and saying ‘yes' to embracing individual differences. We are committed to ensuring that our application process provides an equal employment opportunity to all job seekers, including individuals from diverse gender, cultural and linguistic backgrounds, individuals with a disability, individuals identifying as being part of the LGBTQIA+ community, individuals who may have served in the armed forces or who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander. We also want to do our best to make our recruitment process inclusive.

If you require any adjustments or accessibility support to participate fairly and equitably in the recruitment process, please email AccessibleRecruitment@optus.com.au or call 1800 309 170. We're here to help. When you contact us, please advise your preferred method of contact and we will be in touch within 48 hours.

For more information on Diversity, Inclusion & Belonging at Optus, please visit https://www.optus.com.au/about/inclusion-diversity.

L&D Consultant (6-Month Contract) - Melbourne
Are you an experienced Learning & Development professional looking for a high-impact, enterprise-wide role? We’re seeking a motivated L&D Consultant to join a collaborative, values-led team in Melbourne for a 6-month period, supporting key capability initiatives while a senior team member is on a career break.

About the Organisation
Join a global investment management firm with a strong focus on long-term, responsible investment and sustainable value creation. The organisation operates across Australia, Europe, North America, and Asia, offering a high-performing, international environment. This role sits within a People & Culture team driving significant enterprise-wide capability initiatives and leadership development programs.

The Role
As L&D Consultant, you’ll focus on delivering and executing learning and capability initiatives across the enterprise. This is less about heavy instructional design and more about ensuring programs are embedded effectively, facilitated professionally, and aligned to the organisation’s culture and strategy.

Key responsibilities include:

Leading and supporting enterprise-wide learning and capability projects
Conducting learning needs analysis and translating findings into practical solutions
Managing vendors and procurement processes, including legal coordination
Facilitating workshops on performance reviews, development planning, and feedback conversations
Refreshing and contextualising vendor-provided content to align with organisational culture
Supporting capability framework development at team and business unit level
Contributing to initiatives such as leadership development programs, trust campaigns, purpose and EVP projects, and female representation initiatives
Collaborating effectively across global time zones and with senior stakeholders
What We’re Looking For
Strong project management skills and ability to manage multiple workstreams
High-level stakeholder management capability with credibility at senior levels
Experience in learning needs analysis and program execution
Vendor management and procurement experience, including legal processes
Facilitation skills for leadership and development sessions
Ability to adapt content to suit organisational context
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, high-standards environment
Global exposure and comfort working across cultures and time zones
Practical Details
Contract Duration: 6 months (Melbourne-based)
Salary: $130,000–$150,000 inclusive of super
Hybrid work model: 3-4 days in office
Flexibility for early evening global calls (offset on other days)
Why This Role?
This is an excellent opportunity to work on high-profile enterprise programs, including leadership development, trust initiatives, and culture projects, in a collaborative, values-driven team. You’ll gain exposure to global operations, senior stakeholders, and impactful learning initiatives, all within a premium, supportive workplace.

If you’re a credible, delivery-focused L&D professional ready to step into a dynamic, fast-paced environment, we’d love to hear from you.

We are seeking an experienced Project Lead / Business Analyst to lead the delivery of a project to develop a bespoke application. This role will play a critical part in shaping requirements, guiding the development team, and ensuring the successful delivery of a high-quality solution using Agile/Scrum methodologies.

Working closely with business stakeholders, designers, and developers, you will drive collaboration, manage the product backlog, and ensure the project delivers real business value.
  
Responsibilities

Lead the planning and delivery of a bespoke application development project using Agile/Scrum methodologies.
Facilitate Agile ceremonies including sprint planning, stand-ups, backlog refinement, reviews, and retrospectives.
Engage with stakeholders to gather, document, and prioritise business and functional requirements.
Translate business requirements into user stories, acceptance criteria, and a well-managed product backlog.
Coordinate cross-functional teams including developers, UX/UI designers, and technical specialists to ensure timely delivery.
Track project progress, manage risks and dependencies, and provide regular updates to stakeholders.
Ensure delivered solutions meet business needs, quality standards, and agreed project objectives.
Support user testing, validation, and deployment activities.
Skills & Experience

15 yrs + Proven experience leading Agile/Scrum projects, particularly in software or application development environments.
Strong business analysis capability including requirements elicitation, documentation, and process mapping.
Demonstrated experience managing stakeholders and facilitating workshops with business and technical teams.

Job Title: Customer Engineering Manager, Data Analytics, Google Cloud
Ad Text:
Applicants must have a right to work in Australia as Google is currently unable to sponsor a visa for this position.

At Google, we have a vision of empowerment and equitable opportunity for all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and commit to building reconciliation through Google’s technology, platforms and people and we welcome Indigenous applicants. Please see our Reconciliation Action Plan for more information.

Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: Sydney NSW, Australia; Melbourne VIC, Australia.Minimum qualifications:

Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
10 years of experience with cloud native architecture in a customer-facing or support role.

Experience in pre-sales or field engineering at an enterprise technology company, or similar customer-facing experience.
Experience engaging with, and presenting to, technical stakeholders and executive leaders.
Experience in pre-sales management or people management on a data analytics-related or technical team.
Experience with data analytics technologies or concepts, cloud, and on-premise technologies.
Preferred qualifications:

Experience influencing cross-functional teams (e.g., Product Management, Engineering, Sales), customers, and partners to impact business goals, customer experience, and customer expansion.
Experience tailoring and delivering compelling messages to audiences, asking strategic questions, and leading conversations that drive business opportunity.
Experience in Big Data, including analytics warehousing, data processing, data transformation, data governance, data migrations, ETL, ELT, SQL, NoSQL, performance or scalability optimizations, or batch versus streaming.
Ability to be a thought leader in data analytics and adjacent practice areas, specializing in technical sales and strategizing around customer and industry solutions.
About the job


When leading companies choose Google Cloud, it's a huge win for spreading the power of cloud computing globally. Once educational institutions, government agencies, and other businesses sign on to use Google Cloud products, you come in to facilitate making their work more productive, mobile, and collaborative. You listen and deliver what is most helpful for the customer. You assist fellow sales Googlers by problem-solving key technical issues for our customers. You liaise with the product marketing management and engineering teams to stay on top of industry trends and devise enhancements to Google Cloud products.

Specialist Customer Engineers seek to accelerate Google Cloud customer success by providing domain-specific technical expertise, while responsibly influencing products and solutions.

As a Customer Engineering (CE) Manager, you lead and deploy a team of subject matter experts responsible for working alongside our customers to provide trusted technical and solution advice to accelerate workload migration and remove technical impediments.

Google Cloud accelerates every organization’s ability to digitally transform its business and industry. We deliver enterprise-grade solutions that leverage Google’s cutting-edge technology, and tools that help developers build more sustainably. Customers in more than 200 countries and territories turn to Google Cloud as their trusted partner to enable growth and solve their most critical business problems.

Responsibilities

Lead a team of technical experts in a matrixed organization. Focus on talent strategy, assessing go-to-market readiness and gaps in CE preparedness, skills development, and opportunity coverage to deliver successful cloud transformation outcomes for customers and accelerate business goals.

Execute the technical goal and strategy for your region’s data analytics practice. Lead the broader region's data analytics technical community, to ensure sub-regional technical contributions, achieve scale through artifact and innovation sharing.

Influence cross-functional teams, including Product Management and Engineering, ensuring customer needs are represented in product roadmaps and new technology is incubated and scaled.

Lead technical engagements with strategic customers, working with cross-functional peers to plan customer engagements.

Travel up to 25% to customer sites, conferences, and other related events as required, acting as a public advocate for Google Cloud.

Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

About OccuMED

OccuMED is a fast-growing healthcare business delivering occupational medicine services across Western Australia. We are entering an exciting new phase of growth, underpinned by investment in technology, system transformation, a focus on operational excellence and client service.

This role is a key leadership position within that journey — an opportunity to shape how finance operates in a modern, data-driven healthcare business.

The business is well established with strong revenues and a long history of positive cashflow.

The Opportunity

We are looking for an up-and-coming Finance Manager who is ready to step into a broader leadership role.

This is not a traditional “steady-state” finance role — it is hands-on, commercial, and transformation-focused. You will report and work closely with the CEO and leadership team to build capability, improve processes, and deliver meaningful financial insights that drive business performance.

You will lead and be accountable for the finance function, managing a small team to support this scaling organisation.

Key Responsibilities

Finance Leadership & Team Management

Lead, mentor and develop a small finance team across finance operations

Drive accountability, structure, and capability uplift within the team

Oversee payroll processes to ensure accuracy, compliance, and effective controls

Financial Operations & Systems Transformation

Lead migration from Reckon to Xero

Improve accuracy, timeliness, and efficiency of financial workflows

Reporting & Commercial Insights

Own monthly business performance reporting (labour ratios / operational metrics)

Monthly tax and compliance reporting

Establish daily dashboard reporting

Own cashflow forecasting and working capital management

Lead annual budgeting and forecast models

Oversee debtor management and collections performance

About You

We are looking for someone who is ready to step up, not someone who has been sitting in a long-tenured leadership role.

Ideal Profile:

5–10 years’ experience in a Finance Manager / Senior Accountant / Commercial Analyst role

Strong Xero capability (or proven experience transitioning systems)

Advanced Excel / financial modelling skills

Highly hands-on and operationally minded

Strong commercial acumen — you understand what drives business performance

A natural leader who wants to develop and lead a team

Energised by change, growth, and building something together

Why Join OccuMED?

Be part of a high-growth healthcare business

Work directly with an experienced leadership team, including seasoned operators who have successfully delivered growth and transformation playbooks, offering a fast-track learning environment

Exposure to cutting-edge technology and AI workflows

Apply

If you’re looking for a role where you can step up, lead, and make a real impact, we’d love to hear from you.

Ad Text:
Director, Program Integration Office – Build and Enable from the Ground Up

Are you ready to shape the future and leave your mark? We're looking for a highly experienced and visionary Director to establish and lead a Program Integration Office that will enable the successful delivery of high-impact, enterprise-wide initiatives, driving organisational transformation and operational excellence.

This is your chance to:

Build the PIO from the ground up, establishing the functions, services, and frameworks that empower program and project teams to succeed.
Develop and implement best-practice methodologies, tools, and processesto ensure consistency, quality, and transparency across all initiatives.
Provide expertise in planning, governance, risk management, Program and Project performance, including financial management to ensure programs deliver measurable, sustainable outcomes.
Collaborate with senior leaders and stakeholders to foster cross-sector partnerships and enable informed decision-making.
Create a culture of innovation, accountability, and high performance, mentoring and empowering diverse, multi-disciplinary teams.

Why work with us?

Work alongside passionate professionals in a supportive and inclusive environment that values people and prioritises employee success and wellbeing. This includes:

Competitive salaries

12.75% superannuation
17.5% leave loading

Employee wellbeing

38 hour full time work week
Access to 24/7 confidential employee support providers and counsellors including for immediate family members
Additional flexibility to support your work life balance including access to generous leave entitlements, such as purchased leave, domestic violence leave, reproductive health leave, cultural leave, study and examination leave.

Professional development

Career progression with more than 300 diverse career pathways and career support to take you further!
Access to a variety of programs and initiatives to support training and career growth.
Grow your skills through hands-on experience and access to internal training opportunities and additional financial and leave benefits for approved external training and development.

 Our commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion 

At Queensland Health, our work environment is inclusive and supportive, and we value our employees. We are an equal opportunity employer and encourage applications from people of all cultures, abilities and backgrounds.

Our commitment to cultural safety, equity, diversity and inclusion means we understand some people may need changes to the recruitment process. If you need support during the recruitment process, such as meeting with the panel virtually instead of in person, please reach out to the hiring manager. We value diverse candidates and your need for adjustments will not affect our hiring decisions. 

Ready to apply?

For further information on how to apply please review the attached Role Description.

Experience designing systems and providing expert knowledge in relevant design standards, techniques and methods and tools for large scale complex organisations. (SFIA Skill: DESN: Level 4, METL: 5)
Demonstrated experience in the assessment, analysis, development, documentation and implementation planning of changes for large complex IM/ICT program/project(s). (SFIA Skill: CHMG: Level 4)
